description: Marking an implementation-only dependency PUBLIC leaks it into every consumer, coupling them to internals and breaking when the dependency changes.
body: Failure mode: a dependency needed only to build a library is given PUBLIC visibility, so its includes and link requirements propagate to all consumers that never use it directly. Detection signals: consumers gain access to headers they should not see; removing the internal dependency breaks unrelated downstream targets; the public interface pulls in transitive libraries. Do not mark a build-only dependency PUBLIC to clear a link error. Safer fallback: use PRIVATE for implementation details and INTERFACE or PUBLIC only for what consumers genuinely need, then verify consumers still link.
